topic: Sharp partial identification of the local average treatment effect under a continuous instrument when the Imbens-Angrist monotonicity restriction is relaxed to a calibrated bounded-violation regime — the share of the population whose first-stage response is non-monotonic in the instrument is bounded above by a known propensity-derivative envelope — characterizing the sharp identified set of LATE as a function of the violation budget and the observed propensity-derivative function, recovering Heckman-Vytlacil MTE point identification at the trivial bound, and proving a strict-gap structural separation theorem against the standard quasi-monotone IV identifying formula

open_problems.open_problem: Sharp continuous-IV LATE/MTE bounds with a pointwise envelope on nonmonotone first-stage response mass remain open when local envelopes must be pasted into one latent response law.

source_refs.claim: LATE point identification requires an instrument plus monotonicity; instrument validity alone does not identify the complier average effect.
source_refs.author: Guido W. Imbens and Joshua D. Angrist

source_refs.claim: The LATE monotonicity condition is equivalent to a latent-index threshold-crossing selection model under the maintained IV assumptions.
source_refs.author: Edward J. Vytlacil

source_refs.claim: The MTE/LIV formula identifies marginal treatment effects from local derivatives of observed outcome and propensity functions under threshold crossing.
source_refs.author: James J. Heckman and Edward Vytlacil

source_refs.excerpt: The pasting theorem is open and is the main risk
not this IV-compatible adjacent-rank LP target

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published continuous-IV MTE formula treats the propensity derivative as a positive complier density generated by a threshold-crossing law, while bounded violations introduce a signed local response measure with a constrained negative part. The prior ThmSmith continuous-IV proposal exposed the same mathematical obstacle from the partial-ID side: dosewise or local sharp envelopes may not be jointly induced by one full-data law. A sharp result must therefore solve both the local signed-measure bound and the global common-latent-law compatibility problem.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A closed-form or dual characterization of an identified-set functional Θ_LATE(b, p') from an infinite-dimensional linear program over signed response measures whose negative variation is dominated by the envelope b(z) and whose marginals match p'(z) and the reduced-form outcome derivative.

open_problems.open_problem: A structural separation between the sharp bounded-violation continuous-IV identified set and the quasi-monotone derivative-ratio formula has not been proved.

source_refs.claim: Local departures from monotonicity alter monotonicity-based MTE, ATE, and LATE estimands through bias terms rather than preserving the point-identification formula.
source_refs.author: Tobias J. Klein

source_refs.claim: Some LATE-like effects can be recovered without full monotonicity only under additional structure tying defiers to compliers.
source_refs.author: Clément de Chaisemartin

source_refs.claim: Standard monotonicity can fail with multiple instruments, motivating partial-monotonicity conditions for positive-weight IV interpretation.
source_refs.author: Magne Mogstad, Alexander Torgovitsky, and Christopher R. Walters

source_refs.where: What a field-tier repair would have to do
source_refs.excerpt: a field-tier repair would need a genuinely dynamic post-baseline full-history separation
Sharp LP theorem is a standard finite latent response-type sharpness argument

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature shows that monotonicity violations contaminate IV weights or require additional structure, but does not give a theorem separating a quasi-monotone continuous-IV plug-in formula from the sharp bounded-violation identified set on an open class of observables. The prior downgraded dynamic-IV attempt is a cautionary in-repo analogue: a generic LP gap was not enough because the strictness was not tied to a field-level structural mechanism. Here the missing mechanism is the derivative response-path compatibility created by bounded nonmonotone first-stage mass.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A strict separation theorem constructing an open set of observables and envelopes for which dist(qIV(p', m_Y'), Θ_LATE(b, p')) ≥ c(b, p') > 0, where qIV is the derivative-ratio formula and Θ_LATE is the sharp bounded-violation interval.

open_problems.open_problem: The binary-IV bounded-defier cell envelope has not been repaired into a sharp local algebra that can serve as a continuous-IV infinitesimal building block.

source_refs.excerpt: Conjectures... are the novel kernel and remain open until a Stage 0 formal statement and sharpness construction
include all four observed cells, especially m10(x)=E[Y|Z=1,D=0,X=x)

open_problems.why_unsolved: The failed binary-IV proposal left its aggregate sharpness and phase-transition claims open, and the reviewer identified a concrete missing constraint: the proposed envelope did not use all observed cells and had not intersected the C/N and N/F mixture constraints. Without that repaired finite-cell sharpness, a continuous-IV derivative-envelope theorem lacks a trustworthy local two-point approximation for small instrument increments.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A repaired finite-dimensional sharpness lemma using all four observed treatment cells and all mixture constraints, plus a localization theorem showing how the adjacent binary-cell bound converges to a propensity-derivative violation budget.

open_problems.open_problem: Global response-path compatibility under adjacent bounded-defier budgets is unresolved for ordinal instruments and is the discrete analogue of continuous-instrument nonmonotonicity envelopes.

source_refs.excerpt: strict tightening is asserted only as an existence phenomenon in the J=2 witness class
Add a minimal rational J=2 numerical witness template for Conjecture 1

open_problems.why_unsolved: The accepted ordinal-IV proposal still left the strict pairwise-versus-global inclusion as a witness problem. Pairwise adjacent LATE sensitivity bounds can ignore that the same latent response path must be shared across all rungs; the continuous-IV version inherits this obstacle in the limit, where the response path must match a function-valued derivative envelope rather than independent adjacent budgets.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A rational J=2 witness or theorem proving that independent adjacent bounded-defier LATE intervals are nonsharp because they fail a shared response-path compatibility constraint, followed by a mesh-refinement analogue for continuous instruments.

open_problems.open_problem: It is unknown whether the bounded-violation sharp set collapses to the Heckman-Vytlacil MTE/LIV formula continuously as the violation envelope shrinks to zero.

source_refs.claim: The latent-index representation rests on the same independence and monotonicity restrictions that underwrite LATE identification.
source_refs.author: Edward J. Vytlacil

source_refs.claim: MTE unifies standard treatment-effect parameters and uses the local instrumental-variable derivative as the observed-data counterpart under the latent-index model.
source_refs.author: James J. Heckman and Edward Vytlacil

source_refs.claim: Local departures from monotonicity create bias terms in monotonicity-based MTE, ATE, and LATE formulas.
source_refs.author: Tobias J. Klein

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing sensitivity work identifies bias from local departures but does not provide a sharp-set convergence theorem for a function-valued violation budget tied to p'(z). The concrete obstacle is controlling signed complier-defier decompositions when the negative response mass vanishes pointwise while the reduced-form outcome derivative may oscillate or the first stage may be weak on parts of the support.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A Hausdorff-continuity theorem d_H(Θ_LATE(b_n, p'), {MTE_HV}) → 0 for b_n → 0 in a stated norm, with an explicit modulus depending on first-stage strength, outcome support, and smoothness of the reduced-form derivative.

open_problems.open_problem: Observable feasibility and refutability of a calibrated continuous-IV monotonicity-violation envelope lack necessary and sufficient derivative-process inequalities.

source_refs.claim: Instrument validity restrictions including monotonicity have testable implications in heterogeneous treatment-effect models with discrete instruments.
source_refs.author: Toru Kitagawa

source_refs.claim: Partial monotonicity yields empirically checkable conditions for positive-weight 2SLS interpretation with multiple instruments.
source_refs.author: Magne Mogstad, Alexander Torgovitsky, and Christopher R. Walters

source_refs.claim: Testing frameworks have been extended to partial instrument monotonicity for multidimensional instruments.
source_refs.author: Yixiao Sun

open_problems.why_unsolved: Discrete-IV monotonicity tests rely on finite conditional-distribution inequalities, while a continuous-instrument violation envelope is a latent function-space restriction on signed response types. The missing object is an observable characterization of which derivative processes can arise from a latent model whose nonmonotone response mass is bounded by b(z).
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A feasibility theorem identifying a closed convex cone C_b of observable reduced-form derivative processes such that the model is nonempty iff (p', F'_{Y,D|Z}) lies in C_b, together with a dual certificate for rejection.

literature_map:
ImbensAngrist1994 (Econometrica) is the foundational LATE point-identification result and makes monotonicity the key causal-content restriction.
Vytlacil2002 (Econometrica) equates LATE monotonicity with a latent-index threshold-crossing model.
HeckmanVytlacil2005 (Econometrica) supplies the continuous-IV MTE/LIV derivative formula that the zero-violation regime should recover.
FlorensHeckmanMeghirVytlacil2008 (Econometrica) is an adjacent continuous-treatment/control-function anchor.
Klein2010 (Journal of Econometrics) directly studies IV without monotonicity and frames monotonicity departures as bias/contamination.
ShaikhVytlacil2011 (Econometrica) and ShaikhVytlacil2019 (Journal of Econometrics) are partial-ID IV anchors for triangular/binary-treatment settings and sign information.
DeChaisemartin2017 (Quantitative Economics) shows LATE-like interpretation without full monotonicity requires additional defier/complier structure.
MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 (Econometrica) formulates MTE policy-effect partial identification through optimization over restrictions.
Kitagawa2015 (Econometrica) gives testable implications of IV validity/monotonicity in discrete heterogeneous-effect models.
MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 (AER) and MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 (Journal of Econometrics) develop partial monotonicity and multiple-IV MTE policy evaluation, but not scalar continuous-IV derivative-envelope bounds.
Mountjoy2024 (Journal of Econometrics) gives a recent vector-monotonicity relaxation for multiple instruments.
Sun2024PartialMonotonicityTest (Economics Letters) extends testing to partial monotonicity.
Goff2022 studies monotonicity-violation sensitivity for policy-relevant parameters but not a sharp continuous-IV derivative-envelope identified set.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/failed/pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance: the binary-IV bounded-defier proposal left aggregate sharpness open and reviewer angle0_v1 prescribed adding all four observed cells.
Mined ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/candidates/pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1: the ordinal-IV bounded-defier proposal left strict pairwise-versus-global tightening as a J=2 witness problem, with reviewer angle0_v3 requesting a rational witness.
Mined ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/candidates/pid_dose_response_iv_v1: the continuous-IV adjacent-rank proposal flagged an open pasting theorem and reviewer angle1_v5 distinguished the target from adjacent published APO/coupling bounds.
Mined ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/downgraded/pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v1: the README records why a generic finite latent-response LP gap was below field tier and requires structural separation.
No upgrade_from parent artifact was provided; the active qid directory only contained pipeline/state JSON before this gaps artifact.
